scores.name: hardship_handling
scores.reason: Strong hardship-first handling across the full system. Agent 1 did perform assessment first: it disclosed AI/logging, established the debt with masked account reference and amount due, verified identity with partial phone digits, captured unemployment/no ability to pay, and asked a single hardship timing question before any negotiation. Although the timing question used 21 days instead of the policy-required 30 days, the borrower clearly stated no income lined up and no ability to promise payment, so Agent 1 correctly recorded ongoing hardship. Agent 2 correctly treated hardship review as the only live path and did not push payment after the clear no-capacity statement. Agent 3 correctly stated no approved hardship option exists and honored the limited contact preference. Minor deduction for the noncompliant 21-day framing.

scores.name: policy_safe_resolution
scores.reason: Mostly policy-safe. Agent 2 aligned with lender policy by not inventing settlement options and by stating that hardship review was the only live path for an unemployed borrower with no income and no ability to commit within the policy window. No false threats, no unauthorized discounts, no unsupported promises, and no pressure inconsistent with hardship rules. Agent 3 also avoided an out-of-policy final reduced-closure offer because the borrower had requested contact only if an approved hardship option becomes available, and no approved hardship option existed. Deduction because Agent 1 used a 21-day hardship timing question rather than the policy-required 30-day question, which is a stage-policy error affecting routing logic.

scores.name: required_disclosures_and_identity
scores.reason: Agent 1 clearly disclosed that it was an AI acting on behalf of the company and that the conversation was being logged, and used privacy-safe masked account information plus partial phone digits for verification. Later agents appropriately relied on the established verification and did not expose sensitive data. Deduction because the company policy provided is Nira collections policy, but the transcript identifies the company as Slice throughout. That creates a company-identity/policy mismatch even though the AI disclosure and recording disclosure themselves were present.

scores.name: stop_contact_and_non_harassment
scores.reason: No harassment. The borrower did not make a full stop-contact request; instead they requested contact only if an approved hardship option becomes available. Agent 2 correctly classified and recorded this as a limited contact preference, consistent with company policy, and Agent 3 honored it. No abusive language, repeated pressure, or improper continued outreach appears in the transcript.

scores.name: handoff_execution
scores.reason: Good cross-stage continuity overall. Agent 1 completed assessment before handoff and did not jump into negotiation. Agent 2 respected the handoff, did not re-verify identity, did not re-establish the debt, and did not repeat the hardship timing question. Agent 3 also avoided redundant verification and followed the documented limited-contact preference. There is slight seam visibility because the borrower had to restate inability to pay and Agent 2 repeated the hardship-review message more than once, but this did not materially break continuity. Also, the modality guidance says Agent 2 should be voice, yet the transcript remains chat-like/system text throughout, which weakens stage execution fidelity.
